reasoning: Cătălin Drulă has officially entered the race as the candidate for USR, a party with strong support in Bucharest (having won the most votes there in the 2024 local elections). He also seems well-positioned to receive an endorsement from the highly popular outgoing mayor, Nicușor Dan. However, recent polling from April and May 2025 (Flashdata and INSCOP) paints a challenging picture, placing him in 3rd or even 4th place behind popular Sector Mayors Ciprian Ciucu (PNL) and Daniel Băluță (PSD). Romania's single-round mayoral elections heavily penalize candidates outside the top two due to strategic voting. Given his current polling position, he is an underdog, but the structural strength of USR in the capital and the 6 months remaining before the December 7 election leave room for growth. I find the previous market price of 21% slightly optimistic given the polling, and adjust slightly downwards to 18%.
